text:
"The rational of judgment."... This part of the high priest’s attire, which he wore at his breast, was called the rational of judgment; partly because both priests and people of their duty to God; by carrying the names of all their tribes in his presence; and by the Urim and Thummim, that is, doctrine and truth, which were written upon it: and partly because it gave Divine answers and 
oracles, as if it were rational and endowed with judgment.

"Doctrine and Truth"... Hebrew Urim and Thummim: illuminations and perfections. These words, written on the rational, seem to signify the light of doctrine, and the integrity of life, with which the priests of God ought to approach to him.
